spec: docs/regimes/br/AUTHORITY.md (RegimeSurvey front-matter + body, Phase-1 surveyor artifact)
packaging_note: Brazil's natural 'filing' unit is ONE CANDIDATE's full asset declaration for the 2022 federal cycle: one consulta_cand identity/registration row joined via SQ_CANDIDATO to zero-or-more bem_candidato itemized-asset rows. The manifest schema (agents/workflows/source-exploration.md Phase 2) requires exactly one input.* file per case directory, so each case here packages BOTH source tables as a single input.json: {"consulta_cand": {...one row as an object...}, "bem_candidato": [...that candidate's asset rows, possibly empty...]}. This is a sampler packaging decision, not a source format — spec-writer should treat consulta_cand and bem_candidato as two logically separate source schemas joined at parse time, not a single native TSE format.

source_urls.note: Per-UF URLs of the documented pattern (.../consulta_cand_2022_AL.zip) 404 — confirmed this session (HTTP 404 body, not a policy block). The per-state CSVs ship INSIDE the single nationwide ZIP as one file per UF (e.g. consulta_cand_2022_AC.csv), not as separately downloadable per-UF ZIPs. AUTHORITY.md's documented '[_{UF}].zip' URL pattern is therefore inaccurate for at least the 2022 dataset as currently hosted; flagged back to the SAF (see uf_zip_pattern_correction below) rather than silently worked around.

uf_zip_pattern_correction.finding: docs/regimes/br/AUTHORITY.md's Data catalog section documents per-UF ZIP URLs of the form `.../consulta_cand_{YEAR}_{UF}.zip` / `.../bem_candidato_{YEAR}_{UF}.zip`. This session confirmed by direct HTTP request that `consulta_cand_2022_AL.zip` (and by extension the per-UF pattern generally) returns HTTP 404 with a plain Apache-style 'Not Found' body -- not throttling, not a redirect, a genuine 404. The nationwide `consulta_cand_2022.zip` / `bem_candidato_2022.zip` (no UF suffix) download fine (200, ~4.4MB / ~5.6MB) and contain one CSV per UF inside the ZIP (e.g. `consulta_cand_2022_AC.csv`) plus one nationwide `_BR.csv`/`_BRASIL.csv` and a `leiame.pdf`.
uf_zip_pattern_correction.action_needed: surveyor/write-back: AUTHORITY.md's Data catalog section should be corrected to state the per-UF split is INSIDE the single nationwide ZIP, not a separate downloadable resource per UF. Does not change politeness posture (still 1-2 GETs total per dataset per year) but does change adapter fetch logic: the adapter must download the nationwide ZIP and select the UF-named CSV entries it needs, not construct a per-UF download URL.
uf_zip_pattern_correction.not_yet_amended: This sampler pass did not edit docs/regimes/br/ (out of scope per task instructions -- read-only reference); recorded here for the next AUTHORITY.md write-back.

amendment_timestamp_caveat.finding: Checked DT_ULT_ATUAL_BEM_CANDIDATO distribution across all 27 nationwide bem_candidato_2022 UF files this session (not just AL). Every state has only 2-13 DISTINCT update-timestamp values across its entire candidate population, and in every state 85-99% of ALL rows share ONE dominant date (e.g. AL: 1134/1157 rows all stamped exactly 13/05/2026; SP: 13444/13843 rows all stamped 03/07/2026; MG: 8221/8356 rows all stamped 25/10/2023). Within AL specifically, zero candidates have MIXED per-item update dates -- every candidate's own asset rows are either ALL on the state's dominant late date or ALL on the original ~election-time date; no candidate shows a partial/incremental update. This pattern (one shared timestamp covering nearly an entire state's population, ranging anywhere from weeks-after-election to 2026 depending on the state, with zero per-candidate granularity) reads much more like a BULK backend re-timestamp/reindex event per state (e.g. a data migration or scheduled recompute job that happened to touch the DT_ULT_ATUAL_BEM_CANDIDATO column) than genuine, individually-triggered candidate rectifications as AUTHORITY.md's amendment_mechanism claim currently characterizes it. This does not falsify that per-item amendment CAN happen (the mechanism is judicially documented, per AUTHORITY.md's temasselecionados evidence) -- it means the SPECIFIC evidence cited (SQ_CANDIDATO 20001605787's timestamp) may not be proof of an actual candidate-initiated correction, and the case captured here (amendment_post_election_2026, see below) carries the same caveat.
amendment_timestamp_caveat.flag_for_spec_writer: Before treating a changed DT_ULT_ATUAL_BEM_CANDIDATO as an authoritative supersession trigger (AUTHORITY.md field-mapping table), spec-writer/auditor should decide whether this field is reliable signal at all, given it may reflect an unrelated backend operation rather than a real data change. Recommend treating amendment_post_election_2026 as 'timestamp differs from the file's original ~2022 baseline' rather than 'this candidate personally requested a correction' when writing the parse-strategy edge-case notes. This is an open question, not resolved by this sampler pass -- should be added to AUTHORITY.md's open_questions on next write-back.

politeness.breakdown: 1 HEAD on consulta_cand_2022.zip + 1 HEAD on bem_candidato_2022.zip + 1 GET on each nationwide ZIP (2 GETs) = 4 requests total, each >=2s apart; one failed GET attempt at a non-existent per-UF URL (consulta_cand_2022_AL.zip, 404) preceded these and is included in spirit but was a single lightweight 196-byte 404 response, not counted as a data request

case_selection_rationale: Scoped to two states already sampled by the surveyor (AC, AL) per the task's suggestion, to keep the download bounded while building on already-validated regime understanding. AC supplied the typical case (its bem_candidato timestamps cluster near the election date, giving a clean non-amendment baseline) and the zero-asset edge case (AC's zero-asset population was already the surveyor's own evidence for that quirk). AL supplied the amendment case, since essentially its entire bem_candidato population carries a 2026 timestamp far past the election (see amendment_timestamp_caveat above for an important nuance on what that timestamp likely represents). All three candidates were independently selected this session by directly parsing the raw 2022 CSVs (Node.js, not reused from the surveyor's archived evidence excerpts) and screened for PII-safety in DS_BEM_CANDIDATO before selection: none of the three chosen candidates' asset descriptions contain a street address, vehicle plate, or phone number (screened out other otherwise-eligible candidates, e.g. AL's EUCENIA MARIA VIEIRA, purely because her vehicle rows embed real plates QLC-3432/MUR-7372/NMK-9970).

typical_house_vehicle_land.notes: Straightforward asset mix (motorcycle, small rural house, rural land parcel); all 3 items timestamped at the original ~election-time date (02/10/2022), i.e. NOT touched by the later bulk-timestamp event described in amendment_timestamp_caveat -- a clean non-amendment baseline. DS_BEM_CANDIDATO text mentions only a municipality name (Xapuri, a public Acre city), no street address/plate/phone.

amendment_post_election_2026.notes: Required amendment/correction case -- independently re-derived this session (a DIFFERENT candidate from the surveyor's SQ_CANDIDATO 20001605787 reference instance, per task instructions not to reuse it unverified). All 3 asset items (capital quotas + two VGBL insurance/pension balances) are stamped DT_ULT_ATUAL_BEM_CANDIDATO = 13/05/2026, ~44 months after the 02/10/2022 election. Screened for PII-safety: descriptions reference only company names/CNPJs (not personal data) -- no street address, vehicle plate, or phone. IMPORTANT CAVEAT: see the manifest's amendment_timestamp_caveat block -- AL's bem_candidato file has 1134/1157 rows (98%) sharing this exact same 13/05/2026 date, which reads as a bulk per-state backend re-timestamp event rather than proof this specific candidate personally filed a correction. Captured as the best available real-data instance of a timestamp meaningfully after the election, with this caveat flagged for spec-writer rather than glossed over.

zero_asset_deputado.notes: Required edge case: candidate registered as DEPUTADO FEDERAL, candidacy status APTO (not disqualified/inapt -- a genuinely valid candidacy), with ZERO bem_candidato rows. Exercises AUTHORITY.md's Quirks log finding that 'no bem_candidato rows for this SQ_CANDIDATO' is a legitimate affirmative zero-assets declaration, not a fetch failure or missing data (invariant 6, fail-closed semantics must not trip on this case). bem_candidato is an empty array by construction, not a missing key, so the adapter's join step should treat SQ_CANDIDATO absence in the bem_candidato table as normal.

pii_handling.rationale: Per AUTHORITY.md's personal_data_to_redact and the field-mapping table, CPF is internal-resolution-only and never surfaces past Bronze; voter-registration number and birth date are flagged live PII with no Gold-facing use. None of these three fields are asserted in any future Gold/Silver conformance expectation, so replacing them with clearly-marked synthetic placeholders does not compromise adapter-test meaningfulness while avoiding permanently committing real people's national ID/voter-registration/birth-date data to git history.
pii_handling.ds_bem_candidato_handling: No placeholder substitution applied to any DS_BEM_CANDIDATO value in the 3 captured cases -- all three were selected specifically because their real, verbatim asset descriptions are already free of street addresses, vehicle plates, and phone numbers (this field feeds the Gold asset_description_raw column verbatim per invariant 2, so verbatim-safe real text was preferred over any redaction). No synthetic/constructed DS_BEM_CANDIDATO text was used in any of the 3 cases.
pii_handling.synthetic_case_considered_and_not_added: The task offered discretion to add a 4th case with a synthetic (non-real-person) address-embedded DS_BEM_CANDIDATO to exercise address-in-free-text parsing. Judgment call: not added this pass -- the three required cases (typical/amendment/edge) are all satisfied with clean real data, and address-embedded descriptions are common enough in the wild (observed directly in AL/AC sampling, e.g. the 'RESIDENCIAL IBIZA, BLOCO B, APTO 206' partial-address style seen in an otherwise-typical AC candidate not selected here) that spec-writer/test-designer can decide, with this open question flagged, whether a dedicated address-parsing fixture is worth constructing later. Recorded as an open item rather than silently resolved.

sampler_note_scope: Fixtures only capture the DEPUTADO FEDERAL body this pass (AUTHORITY.md scope also includes SENADOR/suplentes); AC/AL's SENADOR/suplente candidates were screened during selection (see explore.js-equivalent output) but none stood out as more representative than the DEPUTADO FEDERAL picks made. Not a gap in scope coverage -- record_types/body distinction is a DS_CARGO discovery-filter concern per AUTHORITY.md's field mapping, already exercised by the 3 captured DS_CARGO='DEPUTADO FEDERAL' rows; a dedicated SENADOR fixture was judged not required to satisfy the >=3 typical/amendment/edge gate and is left as a builder-discretion addition if the eventual conformance suite wants explicit SENADOR coverage.

expected_outputs.captured_by: test-designer (E2 br Phase 3 second half) -- expected.silver.json/expected.gold.json derived INDEPENDENTLY of any parser (no br adapter logic exists at this point: crates/adapters/br/src has only details.rs + lib.rs, no discover/fetch/parse/normalize), per docs/decisions/automation-policy.md. Row counts below match this manifest's per-case `rows` (3, 3, 0).
expected_outputs.spec: crates/adapters/br/plan.md (field-mapping table, CD_TIPO_BEM_CANDIDATO->AssetClass table, edge cases) + crates/adapters/br/src/details.rs (BrHoldingDetailsV1 contract, committed) -- br has no docs/regimes/<x>.md-style '§4 payload field list' the way us_house/uk_commons_register do, so the Silver payload shape below is this pass's own construction, grounded field-by-field in plan.md's mapping table, not a pre-existing spec.
expected_outputs.silver_shape: expected.silver.json is the serialization of Vec<pipeline::StagingRow>: [{payload, confidence}] -- same wrapper as every prior regime (crates/pipeline/src/adapter.rs StagingRow). payload carries every field plan.md's mapping table says feeds a Gold column or a BrHoldingDetailsV1 field, kept as verbatim source strings (Silver keeps the source's own vocabulary, not the Gold contract) with an added `extractor` string matching every other regime's convention.
expected_outputs.payload_field_list: sq_candidato (join key, verbatim consulta_cand.SQ_CANDIDATO -- politician_id resolution input + filing_id derivation input), dt_eleicao_raw (bem_candidato.DT_ELEICAO, DD/MM/YYYY, -> as_of_date), election_year_raw (ANO_ELEICAO, -> details.election_year), line_item_ordinal_raw (NR_ORDEM_BEM_CANDIDATO, -> details.line_item_ordinal), asset_type_code_raw/asset_type_label_raw (CD_TIPO_BEM_CANDIDATO/DS_TIPO_BEM_CANDIDATO, -> asset_class + details), asset_description_raw (DS_BEM_CANDIDATO, verbatim, invariant 2), value_raw (VR_BEM_CANDIDATO comma-decimal string, pre-parse), last_updated_date_raw/last_updated_time_raw (DT_ULT_ATUAL_BEM_CANDIDATO/HH_ULT_ATUAL_BEM_CANDIDATO, forensic-only per plan.md edge case 2), extractor.
expected_outputs.payload_shape_flag: plan.md's own 'Row unit' section explicitly leaves 'the exact join-field set' as 'a rust-builder implementation decision, not fully specified here' -- so, UNLIKE us_house/uk_commons_register (where an earlier regime-spec phase already pinned the exact StagingRow payload field list before test-designer ran), this pass is itself minting that shape for the first time. FLAGGED for auditor/rust-builder: the field list above is a defensible, mapping-table-grounded proposal, not a locked contract -- if rust-builder's real parse() needs additional passthrough fields (e.g. for logging/debugging), that is additive and does not contradict these expected values, since the Gold/details assertions only depend on the fields plan.md actually maps.
expected_outputs.pii_excluded_from_silver_flag: Per the task's explicit instruction ('PII fields... you don't need them for expected outputs'), this pass omits NR_CPF_CANDIDATO, NR_TITULO_ELEITORAL_CANDIDATO, and DT_NASCIMENTO from the Silver payload entirely, not just from Gold/details. TENSION FLAGGED: plan.md's own 'Row unit' section recommends normalize() needs 'NR_TITULO_ELEITORAL_CANDIDATO/NR_CPF_CANDIDATO for politician resolution' threaded through the payload -- this pass took the more conservative reading (omit everywhere) rather than guessing the task intended 'omit from Gold/details only, but still carry the (already-synthetic-placeholder) PII through Silver.' DS_CARGO was excluded for a separate reason: plan.md calls it 'a discovery-time filter, not a Gold column', i.e. consumed before parse() produces StagingRows, not needed by normalize(). Auditor should confirm which reading rust-builder should follow; either way, it is additive to the payload shape and does not change any asserted Gold/Silver value in this pass's fixtures.
expected_outputs.row_order_assumption_flag: typical_house_vehicle_land's 3 rows are asserted in the SAME order as input.json's bem_candidato array (moto/ordinal-3, casa/ordinal-1, terreno/ordinal-2) -- i.e. source/file order, NOT re-sorted by NR_ORDEM_BEM_CANDIDATO. Nothing in plan.md specifies a sort step, and a straight CSV-row-emission parser (the documented parse strategy) would not introduce one; amendment_post_election_2026 happens to already be ordinal-sorted in its source file (1,2,3) so it doesn't disambiguate this assumption. FLAGGED for rust-builder/auditor: if the real parser sorts by ordinal instead, only typical_house_vehicle_land's row ORDER (not content) would need re-deriving.
expected_outputs.extractor_convention: 'br_bem_candidato/csv@1' for every row's payload.extractor and Gold extracted_by -- an invented convention (this is the first br fixture pass) mirroring the cross-regime '{regime_or_doctype}/{mechanism}@{version}' pattern (us_house_ptr/text@1, uk_commons_register/api@1). No LLM-fallback seam exists for this regime's core parse (plan.md), so there is no '/llm@1' variant to reserve.
expected_outputs.confidence_literals: Every row in both non-empty cases scores the JSON float literal 1.0 (never integer 1 -- serde_json deep-compares PosInt(1) and Float(1.0) unequal, per the us_senate/uk_commons_register convention). No penalty applied to amendment_post_election_2026's 3 rows (codes 32/97) -- see asset_class_confidence_judgment_call below for the reasoning and the plan.md inconsistency this pass found while deciding it.
expected_outputs.asset_class_confidence_judgment_call: plan.md's own CD_TIPO_BEM_CANDIDATO->AssetClass table marks BOTH code 32 (->Private) and code 97 (->Other) 'High (post-audit)' confidence in the per-code Confidence column -- i.e. the audit fully resolved these, not merely defaulted them. Immediately below that table, though, plan.md's 'Recommended implementation shape' paragraph (written, by its own text, for a NOT-YET-BUILT rust-builder lookup) still describes routing 32/97 through the lookup's `None` branch with 'the caller bucketing None to AssetClass::Other plus an extraction-confidence penalty' -- reading that literally would MISCLASSIFY code 32 as Other (contradicting the resolved Private mapping two paragraphs above in the same file), since the paragraph never carves out a special case for 32 landing anywhere but the None->Other default. This pass's judgment call: treat the per-code 'High (post-audit)' rating as the authoritative, current status (a lookup table built with 5 confidently-audited entries is no less reliable at run time than one with 3, regardless of how much research went into confirming the extra 2) and score confidence 1.0 uniformly rather than invent an unpin-pointed penalty magnitude for a paragraph that -- read literally -- would ship a classification bug. FLAGGING BOTH: (1) the confidence-scoring choice itself, since 'apply a small penalty instead' is an equally defensible alternative reading and the task explicitly invited either answer; (2) the more consequential finding that plan.md's 'Recommended implementation shape' paragraph, if implemented as literally written, would misclassify code 32 as Other instead of Private -- flagged for auditor/rust-builder to reconcile in plan.md or the eventual tables.rs-equivalent before implementation, independent of this pass's confidence-scoring decision.
expected_outputs.holding_gold_shape: first `holding` record_type adapter in this project: side/transaction_date/notified_date NULL on every row (GoldCandidate::validate() requires only as_of_date for RecordType::Holding, confirmed by reading crates/core/src/domain/gold.rs directly -- Holding's match arm requires nothing else, unlike Transaction's side+transaction_date pair); owner NULL on every row (no owner/spouse/dependent concept exists in this regime, per plan.md -- None, not Owner::Unknown, matches the resolved decision in this pass's task brief); instrument_id NULL (invariant 3, no ISIN/ticker/registry id in source); fingerprint NULL (computed at promotion, Task 6 convention).
expected_outputs.money_strings: All Gold value bounds are decimal STRINGS, low==high (exact, no banding per AUTHORITY.md value_precision), currency BRL: '15000.00', '10000.00', '900000.00', '1500.00', '5300.00', '8213.04'.
expected_outputs.verification_method: Hand-verification via a THROWAWAY integration test (crates/adapters/br/tests/scratch_validate_expected.rs, created, run, then deleted -- not a committed artifact, per task instructions not to touch crates/adapters/br/src/details.rs or add permanent files): deserialized every expected.gold.json row into govfolio_core::domain::gold::GoldCandidate, called .validate() (all passed), asserted the Holding-specific None/Some shape above, deserialized every row's `details` value into br::details::BrHoldingDetailsV1 and confirmed a byte-exact re-serialization round-trip plus exactly 9 fields, and cross-checked the 5 asset_class assignments against the resolved code table. `cargo test -p br` (6 tests: 2 committed details.rs unit tests + this pass's 4 throwaway tests) was green before the throwaway file was deleted; `git status --porcelain crates/adapters/br` confirmed no residue afterward.

conformance_ids.rationale: The fixtures carry no ULIDs and conformance runs with pool: None, so filing/politician/regime ids cannot be resolved from a roster -- same convention as us_house/uk_commons_register. Prefix is 0BRA* (Brazil's ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code; all of B/R/A are valid Crockford base32 letters, unlike UK's 'U' which is why uk_commons_register used 0GBR* instead of 0UK*). Filing ids embed ANO_ELEICAO + SQ_CANDIDATO (in that order, zero-padded) rather than SQ_CANDIDATO alone, because plan.md's field-mapping table explicitly flags that 'FilingRef.external_id derived from SQ_CANDIDATO (+ year, since SQ_CANDIDATO is only unique within one cycle's file set)' -- all 3 fixtures happen to be 2022, so this doesn't disambiguate anything in THIS fixture set, but keeps the constant-minting scheme correct for a future cross-cycle fixture. Every id verified 26 chars and matches the ^[0-7][0-9A-HJKMNP-TV-Z]{25}$ pattern from crates/core/src/ids.rs.
conformance_ids.politician_key_note: keys are the numeric SQ_CANDIDATO as a string (this cycle's join key, AUTHORITY.md identifiers_available -- NOT a durable cross-cycle politician key on its own; pool-backed production runs resolve the real politician_id via NR_TITULO_ELEITORAL_CANDIDATO/NR_CPF_CANDIDATO instead, per plan.md).
conformance_ids.filing_id_key_note: keys are the same SQ_CANDIDATO strings as politician_ids above (one candidate == one filing == one FilingRef in this regime, plan.md 'MANIFEST.json packaging note'); the zero_asset_deputado id (10001606131) is unused by any asserted Gold/Silver value (its case emits zero rows) but is minted for completeness in case a future conformance harness wants to assert the discover()/parse() call for that candidate specifically returns zero StagingRows rather than skipping the candidate entirely.
